dimensions.rationale: The response effectively grounds recommendations in the user's specific childcare and HSA transactions.
The response uses the stale $5,000 DCFSA limit instead of the 2026 $7,500 limit specified in the locked facts.

The response provides actionable steps but misses the opportunity to explicitly mention the Salesforce-specific wellness reimbursement or the specific Salesforce 401(k) match details provided in the locked facts.
The response appropriately caveats the need to verify plan-level benefits and consult tax advisors.

factualClaims.claim: 2026 HSA catch-up contribution for age 55+ is $1,000

factualIssues: The response cites the stale $5,000 DCFSA limit instead of the 2026 $7,500 limit.
missedOpportunities: Did not mention the Salesforce $1,200 annual wellness reimbursement.
Did not mention the Salesforce $750 HSA seed contribution.
Did not explicitly reference the Salesforce 401(k) 6% match cap.
summary: The response provides helpful, grounded advice but fails to use the correct 2026 dependent care FSA limit and misses several specific Salesforce employer benefits.
